rag.description: The ReadPdfFile plugin extracts text from PDF and plain text files using a file path, applies a regular expression to filter the content, and stores the matched result in a session parameter as Base64. It keeps workflows reliable by delivering extracted document content cleanly for downstream steps.

qa.question: What does ReadPdfFile do?
qa.answer: ReadPdfFile reads a PDF or plain text file from a given path, extracts the full text content, and stores it in a session parameter for downstream use.

qa.question: What parameters does ReadPdfFile accept?
qa.answer: It accepts Argument (the file path, mandatory) and RegularExpression (an optional filter applied to extracted text, defaulting to `(?si).*` which matches all content).

qa.question: Where can I find the ReadPdfFile source code and documentation?
What is the best practice when using ReadPdfFile in a workflow?
qa.answer: Provide a valid file path in Argument and a targeted RegularExpression to isolate only the content you need; reference ReadPdfFile:Content in downstream steps to reuse the result without re-reading the file.

qa.question: What output does ReadPdfFile produce?
qa.answer: The matched text is stored in the session parameter ReadPdfFile:Content and the full raw content is exposed through the entity field Content.

qa.question: What is the plugin key, type, version, category, platform, and author for ReadPdfFile?
qa.answer: Key: ReadPdfFile, pluginType: Action, version: 4, category: DataExtraction, platform: Any, author: Roei Sabag (https://www.linkedin.com/in/roei-sabag-247aa18/).

qa.question: What is the ReadPdfFile plugin summary?
qa.answer: Reads PDF and plain text files from a given file path, applies a regular expression to filter the extracted text, converts the matched portion to Base64, and stores the result in a session parameter for downstream reuse.

qa.question: What happens if the regular expression does not match any content?
qa.answer: When no match is found the result falls back to an empty string; the plugin completes without throwing an error.

qa.question: Why is the extracted content stored as Base64?
qa.answer: Base64 encoding ensures the extracted text can be safely transported and stored even when it contains special characters, line breaks, or binary sequences.

qa.question: What is the entity field Content?
qa.answer: The entity field Content holds the full raw text extracted from the file before any regular expression filtering. Access it when you need the unfiltered content alongside the filtered session parameter.

qa.question: What happens if the file does not exist at the given path?
qa.answer: The plugin raises an exception and adds it to the response error list. Workflow continuation depends on the runner's failure-handling configuration.

qa.question: What happens if a non-PDF file is provided?
qa.answer: Non-PDF files bypass PdfPig and are read directly as plain text using the system default encoding. The same regular expression filtering and Base64 conversion apply.

qa.question: How do I reference the extracted content in downstream steps?
qa.answer: Use the session parameter key ReadPdfFile:Content. Decode the Base64 value before use if you need the plain text string.

description: ### Purpose
Reads PDF and plain text files from a file path and extracts their full text content.
PDF files are processed page by page using PdfPig; all other file types are read directly as plain text.
A regular expression filters the extracted content, and the matched result is converted to Base64 for safe transport and storage.
Makes it straightforward to pull specific data from documents inside automation workflows without custom code.
### Key Features and Functionality
| Feature                      | Description                                                                           |
| PDF Text Extraction          | Reads all pages of a PDF using PdfPig and joins word tokens into a single text block. |
| Plain Text File Reading      | Reads non-PDF files directly using the system default encoding.                       |
| Regular Expression Filtering | Applies a regex to the extracted content to isolate a specific portion of the text.   |
| Base64 Encoding              | Converts the matched content to Base64 for safe transport and reuse.                  |
| Session Storage              | Saves the extracted result in a session parameter for use by downstream steps.        |
### Usages in RPA
| Use Case                 | Description                                                                                 |
| Invoice Data Extraction  | Pull invoice numbers, totals, or dates from PDF invoices during document processing runs.   |
| Report Parsing           | Read summary sections from generated PDF reports and pass values to downstream steps.       |
| Text File Processing     | Extract specific lines or values from plain text log or configuration files.                |
| Document Content Routing | Read file content and store it in session parameters for further classification or routing. |
### Usages in Automation Testing
| Use Case                 | Description                                                                                       |
| PDF Content Assertion    | Verify that a generated PDF contains expected text such as a confirmation number or status label. |
| Report Validation        | Read values from exported PDF reports and compare them against expected test data.                |
| File Output Verification | Check that a workflow-generated text file contains the correct content after processing.          |
| Regex Match Testing      | Confirm that specific patterns are present in extracted file content as part of test assertions.  |
Contains the full text content extracted from the PDF or text file.
Holds the complete raw text exactly as it appears after page extraction or file reading.
Access it when unfiltered content is needed alongside any downstream processing.
entity.name: Content

edge_cases: File does not exist at the given path
File is not a valid PDF
annotations.expected_result: All text from the PDF is extracted and stored in the session parameter ReadPdfFile:Content.
annotations.notes: The argument must be a valid path to a PDF file. PdfPig reads each page and joins word tokens into a single string. The default regular expression matches all extracted content.

description: ### Extract Full Text from a PDF File
Reads all pages of the PDF at the given path using PdfPig and joins word tokens into a single text block.
No custom regular expression is supplied, so the default `(?si).*` pattern matches all extracted content.
The full text is stored in the session parameter `ReadPdfFile:Content` for use in downstream steps.

edge_cases: File extension is not .pdf; plain text branch is used instead of PdfPig
Pattern match is partial, returning only part of the expected content
Regular expression does not match any content
annotations.expected_result: Only the portion of the extracted content matching the regular expression is converted to Base64 and stored in ReadPdfFile:Content.
annotations.notes: When regularExpression is set, only the first match is used. Non-PDF files bypass PdfPig and are read as plain text; the same regex applies regardless of file type.

description: ### Extract Filtered Content Using a Regular Expression
Reads the file at the given path and applies a regular expression to the extracted text.
A regular expression `Invoice\s+#\d+` is applied to the extracted content to isolate matching invoice identifiers.
The first match is converted to Base64 and stored in the session parameter `ReadPdfFile:Content`.
ReadPdfFile:Content holds the full text content extracted from the PDF or plain text file.
Reference it in downstream steps to reuse the content without re-reading the source file.
Set every time the plugin runs, regardless of the regular expression configured.
outputParameters.name: ReadPdfFile:Content

description: Specifies the path to the file to read.
PDF files are processed with PdfPig to extract text from every page.
All other file types are read as plain text using the system default encoding.
properties.name: Argument

description: Applies a regular expression to the extracted file content to isolate a specific portion.
Defaults to `(?si).*` when omitted, which selects the entire extracted text.
If no match is found, the result is set to an empty string.
properties.name: RegularExpression

summary: Reads PDF and plain text files from a given file path and extracts their full text content.
Applies a regular expression to the extracted text and converts the matched portion to Base64.
Stores the result in a session parameter for reuse in downstream workflow steps.
